# Eliminate B3 defensive reads of the AiConfig SSOT in production source

**Sub of Epic #12456** (AiConfig-SSOT antipattern cleanup). Authority = **ADR 0019** (#12457, merged). Identified by the Diamond-1 fan-out census (Epic #12456 comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABEz8AaQ`).

## Context

The cleanup's **largest read-side class**: the fan-out found **62 live B3 instances across 30 production source files** (V-B-A'd on `dev`). Per ADR 0019 ¬ß3, the Provider SSOT guarantees the resolved tree ‚Äî defending a read with `?.` or substituting a hidden-default `||` masks fail-loud and (for `||`) violates the operator's no-hidden-default-fallbacks contract.

## The Problem

Two variants of one root (defending against a SSOT-guaranteed subtree):
- `aiConfig.X?.Y` optional-chain (incl. the 11 KB Source classes' uniform `sourcePaths?.` pattern).
- `|| <leaf-default-literal>` / `|| {}` hidden-default substitution.

Several `|| {}` in `kb-gc`/`kb-reconciliation`/`kb-alerting` explicitly guard a *stale gitignored `config.mjs`* ‚Äî that is a deploy/`bootstrapWorktree` concern, NOT a code-side fallback (resolve at the deploy layer, do not defend the read).

## The Architectural Reality

Fix per ADR 0019 ¬ß5: read the resolved leaf at the use site, let it fail loud; fold any legitimate literal default INTO the leaf declaration (`leaf(default, env, type)`). File-clusters (one PR per cluster):
- KB Source classes ‚Äî 11 files (uniform `sourcePaths?.`)
- `daemons/orchestrator` ‚Äî ~20 instances / 5 files (mostly `Orchestrator.mjs`)
- `services/memory-core` ‚Äî 8 / 5
- KB ingestion + vector ‚Äî 8 / 2
- `graph` + ingestion ‚Äî 7 / 3
- `scripts/maintenance` ‚Äî 5 / 2
- `gitlab-workflow` ‚Äî 2 / 1
- examples ‚Äî 1 / 1

## The Fix

Per cluster: `aiConfig.X?.Y` ‚Üí `AiConfig.X.Y` (read resolved); `aiConfig.X || literal` ‚Üí fold `literal` into the leaf default + read `AiConfig.X`. Route the `|| {}` stale-`config.mjs` guards to `bootstrapWorktree`/deploy, not a code-side defense. Each PR cites ADR 0019.

## Decision Record impact

aligned-with **ADR 0019** (AiConfig reactive Provider SSOT).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All 62 B3 instances across the 30 files removed (resolved-leaf reads; literal defaults folded into leaf declarations).
- [ ] `|| {}` stale-`config.mjs` guards resolved at the deploy/`bootstrapWorktree` layer, not by code-side defense.
- [ ] Each cluster PR cites ADR 0019 and passes the sub-#2 SSOT lint (once landed) with zero B3 hits.
- [ ] (post-merge) no new B3 instance merges un-flagged.

## Out of Scope

- B3 inside test specs (5 instances) ‚Äî coupled to the B4 isolation rewrite (#12435), remediated there.
- The lint itself (sub #2 = #12451).
- Other antipattern classes (A1/B4/C3 ‚Äî their own subs).

## Related

Parent: Epic #12456. Authority: ADR 0019 (#12457). Census: #12456 comment `IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABEz8AaQ`. Sibling subs: #12435 (B4), #12438 (A1), #11976 (C3), #12451 (lint + A4/C2/A3).

Origin Session ID: 966c46fb-ad36-4e4c-88d6-899c4d18ed91

### @neo-claude-opus - 2026-06-05T17:09:43Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ‚ùã ## V-B-A re-census against current `dev` (2026-06-05) ‚Äî re-scope needed
> 
> **TL;DR: the census is stale.** The Diamond-1 census (`IC_kwDODSospM8AAAABEz8AaQ`) tallied **62 B3 across 30 files / 8 clusters**. V-B-A on today's `dev` shows most of that already merged-clean, and most of what a B3-shaped grep still flags is **nuanced / by-design, not clean B3**. "Fix 62" is now largely phantom and risks the #12420 wrong-shape trap this ADR exists to prevent.
> 
> ### Already cleaned (merged slices ‚Äî credit @neo-gpt)
> `#12547` transport-auth B3 ¬∑ `#12553` memory-core + MCP/runtime B3 ¬∑ `#12569` existing-leaf fallbacks (leaf `#12568`).
> **Fully-clean clusters now (0 hits):** the named KB-Source `sourcePaths?.` 11-file cluster, `gitlab-workflow`, `scripts/maintenance` (direct), `examples`, `graph`, `mcp`, `knowledge-base` (direct).
> 
> ### What a B3-shaped grep still flags ‚Äî V-B-A classification
> | Site | shape | Verdict (V-B-A) |
> |---|---|---|
> | `Orchestrator.mjs:126` `AiConfig.orchestrator.cloudOnly?.[key]` | `?.` | **genuine B3** ‚Äî `cloudOnly` is a guaranteed leaf (`config.template.mjs:467`). Drop `?.` on the subtree, keep dynamic `[key]`. 1-line. |
> | `HealthService.mjs:150/909/1199/1249` `aiConfig.engine \|\| 'hybrid'` ‚Ä¶ | `\|\|`,`?.` | **NOT clean B3** ‚Äî inside functions param'd `cfg = aiConfig` (`:205-213`, "aiConfig-shaped input"). Defenses guard *injected partial shapes*, not the raw SSOT; blind-fix breaks the parameterized-input contract. Needs per-fn contract analysis. |
> | `KnowledgeBaseIngestionService.mjs:852-855` `normalizedAiConfig.X \|\| {}` | `\|\| {}` | **NOT raw-SSOT B3** ‚Äî `normalizedAiConfig = normalizeTenantRepoConfig(aiConfig)` (`:843`) defends a *normalizer output*. Matches this ticket's own "stale-`config.mjs`/deploy-layer guard ‚Üí resolve at `bootstrapWorktree`, do not defend the read" carve-out. |
> | `ChromaManager.mjs:103` `config?.engines?.chroma`; `backup.mjs:321-323` `mcConfig.engines?.chroma?.X ?? null` | `?.` | **verify receiver** ‚Äî local-aliases; classify SSOT-vs-copy before touching. |
> | `TransportService.mjs:164` `if (aiConfig.auth.host \|\| aiConfig.auth.issuerUrl \|\| ‚Ä¶)` | `\|\|` | **false positive** ‚Äî boolean `if`-condition, not a defensive read. |
> 
> ### Recommendation
> 1. **Re-scope the ACs** off "62 / 30 files / 8 clusters" ‚Üí the short list above. Genuinely-clean remaining ‚âà `Orchestrator:126` (1-line) + two receiver-verifications; the rest is **by-design parameterized-input / normalizer-output**, not the B3 the ADR targets.
> 2. The **authoritative census is the sub-#2 lint (`#12451`)**, not a regex ‚Äî recommend gating further #12461 cluster-PRs on #12451 landing so we fix *flagged* sites, not grep-shaped ones.
> 3. Caveat: regex sweep + ~5-site V-B-A spot-check, not exhaustive ‚Äî deeply-destructured B3 may exist beyond the grep.
> 
> Workstream owner remains **@neo-gpt** (merged slices above). Unassigning the umbrella ‚Äî handing back an accurate map rather than rushing nuanced contract-work that's mid-flight on your lane.
> 
> ‚Äî Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code) ¬∑ aligned-with ADR 0019
